landscape_summary: Week 21 was defined by two stories. (1) MyFitnessPal's redesign backlash is hardening into a trust crisis — three weeks after the new Today tab shipped, the company has confirmed there will be no rollback (PiunikaWeb, May 5) and users are exploiting a region-switch workaround to keep the old UI; meanwhile MFP's March acquisition of teen-built Cal AI ($30M ARR, 15M downloads) and the March launch of MyFitnessPal Ads (a wellness retail-media network) make clear the strategic direction is AI-monetization-first, user-trust-later. (2) Noom's microdose GLP-1 program dropped its BMI eligibility floor from 25 to 21 this week and bundled a 17-biomarker home-test kit at $149/mo, while also acquiring a compounding pharmacy — Noom is no longer in our reference set as a consumer tracker; it is a vertically integrated longevity-medicine company. Yazio and Lose It shipped maintenance-only releases. Lifesum continues to roll its chat-style AI Tracking but App Store reviews flag misidentification (cashews → shrimp) and auto-renewal billing disputes. The cross-app review pattern is unmistakable: intrusive monetization, paywalled-once-free features, and dopamine-loop gamification are bleeding trust across every competitor. A calm, Hebrew-first, ad-free product is more defensible than it was two weeks ago.

bullets: Redesign backlash hardens: company confirmed (PiunikaWeb, May 5) the new Today tab is 'the path forward' with no rollback — users are switching account region to Turkey as a workaround. Trust damage is now visible in App Store reviews.

Cal AI acquisition (announced Mar 2, 2026 per TechCrunch) — bought the viral teen-built photo-calorie app (15M downloads, $30M ARR) and is integrating it into MFP's 20M-item food database. AI-logging is now MFP's flagship.
MyFitnessPal Ads launched Mar 17, 2026 (GlobeNewswire) — wellness retail-media network monetizing the user base for brand advertisers. User-experience cost is real; reviews already cite ad density.
Maintenance week: v12.83.1 → 12.85.1 shipped (May 15-21, APKMirror) with generic 'further improvements' release notes — no headline features.
Still leaning on the 'AI Calorie Tracker' rebrand and the IF + photo + barcode bundle as the durable wedge.
Influx Inc. partnership for multilingual support (LeadIQ) signals international scaling — Hebrew is NOT yet on their roadmap.
Mid-May 2026 release was bug fixes only (Uptodown) — feature velocity has stalled.

The 2026 paywall move (barcode 'Scan It' + macro tracking now Premium-only for new users, per NutriScan) is generating sustained backlash in reviews; opportunity for a free Hebrew-first scanner remains wide open.
Voice meal logging ('Say It') + single-photo full-meal AI logging remain the marketed AI features (FitBudd).

BIGGEST move of the week: microdose GLP-1 program dropped BMI eligibility from 25 to 21 (eMarketer, MobiHealthNews) — 'the vast majority of US adults' is now the TAM. $149/mo includes at-home 17-biomarker test kits.

Also launched a $129 compounded GLP-1 SKU with industry-first 'taper-off guarantee' positioning (Noom press), and acquired a compounding pharmacy (MobiHealthNews) for supply-chain vertical integration.

Repositioning from 'weight loss app' to 'proactive whole-health / longevity medicine' (Fitt Insider). Noom is no longer a consumer-tracker competitor — they have left the field. Removes pressure from one direction; signals the consumer-tracker category is being abandoned by the premium player.
AI Tracking (chat-style multimodal entry) continuing its May 2026 rollout — Premium-gated; users can toggle back to Classic Tracking under Customize Diary (Lifesum Helpdesk).

Accuracy collapse in App Store reviews: cashews identified as shrimp, mug-in-photo logged as coffee, barcode scans doubling/tripling calories (NutriScan, Trustpilot). Validates W20's 'edit before confirm' guidance.

Billing-trust collapse: Trustpilot flooded with auto-renewal complaints, PayPal charges continuing after in-app cancellation. Universal complaint pattern across MFP / Noom / Lifesum — predatory monetization is the category-wide failure mode of 2026.

trends.trend: Protein-first targeting is replacing calorie-deficit as the primary daily metric
trends.evidence: Welling 2026 protein-tracking roundup, Fuel macro-tracking 2026 review — both explicitly tied to the GLP-1 era ('eat less → must hit protein to preserve lean mass'). Noom's microdose program leads with biomarker + protein targeting; MFP and Lifesum macros surface protein at the top of their cards in 2026 redesigns.
trends.relevance: HIGH for Israeli home cooks. Israeli diet is carb-heavy (pita, rice, bourekas, malabi); a 'did you hit your protein today?' daily check is more actionable and less moralistic than calorie counting. Better fit for the תזונאי target user (macro-curious, not bodybuilder) than a calorie-deficit dashboard.

trends.evidence: Envato UX/UI 2026 trends and UXPilot mobile design 2026 both explicitly call out gamification fatigue and recommend 'calm micro-interactions' + AI-as-copilot over AI-as-autopilot. MFP redesign backlash (PiunikaWeb, May 5) is the live consumer evidence: streaks + restructured macros are NOT universally loved.
trends.relevance: HIGH and a direct counterpoint to W20's 'pin a streak counter' recommendation. Streaks shame the Shomer Shabbat user who doesn't log Saturday. Streaks should remain a feature but OFF BY DEFAULT, opt-in only, with a calm-mode pledge as a positioning differentiator.

trends.trend: GLP-1 companion features still substantive, not hype
trends.evidence: Noom's BMI-21 microdose launch this week (eMarketer, MobiHealthNews) plus pharmacy acquisition; Shotsy 3.0 (March 2026) added oral GLP-1 tracking; MFP's GLP-1 module (W20). Israeli context: Ozempic/Wegovy uptake via Kupot Holim is rising — real demand exists locally, though smaller than US scale.
trends.relevance: MEDIUM. Most תזונאי users are macro-curious cooks, not patients. Worth a lightweight 'are you on a GLP-1 med?' opt-in flag that auto-raises the protein floor (lean-mass preservation) — NOT a full medication-tracking surface, which is RED-tier (PHI) and out of scope.

ignore_list.trend: Wearables-unified dashboards (e.g. Vora Health, 500+ wearable integrations)
ignore_list.reason: Wrong audience. Israeli home cooks don't wear Whoop straps. 6+ months of integration work with no PMF for our segment. Ignore.
Monetization that breaks user trust (per W21 review patterns) is exactly what our anti-position is selling against. Even at scale, do not pursue.
Still no PMF in nutrition. Users don't want their cousin seeing they ate three malabis. Hype with no substance — same call as Q1.

recommendations.what: Add a 'protein-of-the-day' widget to the home tab, surfaced ABOVE the calorie ring. Show: protein g consumed vs personalized floor (1.6 g/kg lean body mass, derived from existing Agent 2 BMR/TDEE inputs), color-coded ring (red < 70%, yellow 70-90%, green >= 90%), and a 'מה לאכול להשלים?' (what to eat to close the gap) suggestion that pulls from Agent 4 inventory and Agent 5 planner. Keep the existing calorie dashboard accessible via a tab toggle — do not delete it; promote protein to the primary daily metric. Hebrew label: 'חלבון להיום'.
recommendations.inspired_by: Welling 2026 protein-tracking roundup; Fuel macro-tracking 2026; Noom's biomarker-led GLP-1 program (premium signal); MFP/Lifesum 2026 macro cards surfacing protein first.
recommendations.tier_reason: New widget on the home tab, surfaces existing Agent 2 macro targets in a new way, requires a small user-profile field (lean body mass estimate or carry-over from BMR inputs). Feature-flagged so the old calorie-primary view remains the default for existing users.
recommendations.why_it_matters: Israeli diet is carb-heavy (pita, rice, bourekas, hummus); calorie counting is moralistic and exhausting for home cooks. Protein-floor framing is positive ('add one more egg') vs negative ('you went over'). Lower cognitive load, fits the target user (macro-curious cooks, not bodybuilders or fitness influencers), and sets up REC 3 (GLP-1 protein-floor flag). Also: this is the macro that matters for Neo's hedge-fund-era 'efficiency' framing — protein per shekel is the unit-economics metric for groceries.
recommendations.user_segment: Macro-aware home cooks who feel guilt-fatigued by calorie tracking; ~70% of the install base per W20 segmentation.

build_hand_off.owner_agent: Agent 2 (Nutrition) + Agent 5 (Planner) + Agent 1 (Contracts) for LBM field
build_hand_off.schema_change: minor — add user_profile.lean_body_mass_kg (nullable, derived) and user_preferences.primary_metric ('calories'|'protein', default 'calories' for existing users, 'protein' for new). Non-breaking.
build_hand_off.metrics: % users toggling primary_metric to 'protein'; protein-floor hit-rate D7/D30; 'close the gap' suggestion click-through; retention delta for protein-primary cohort vs calorie-primary cohort

recommendations.what: Convert all gamification (streaks, badges, daily nudges, weekly summary push) to OPT-IN. Default state for new and existing users is 'Calm Mode': no streak counter on home, no fire-emoji shaming, no auto-enabled push notifications. Add a one-screen Settings → 'מצב שקט' (Calm Mode) toggle group with three controls: (a) הצג סטריקים (show streaks) — off by default, (b) התראות יומיות (daily reminders) — off by default, (c) שלח סיכום שבועי (weekly summary email) — off by default. Add a small banner on the home tab the first time the user opens the app after this ship: 'אצלנו אין סטריקים. אין פרסומות. אין עונשים. אתה בשליטה.' (We have no streaks, no ads, no punishments. You are in control.). Position this externally as a product stance.
recommendations.inspired_by: Envato UX/UI 2026 + UXPilot mobile 2026 calm-UI trend; MyFitnessPal redesign backlash (PiunikaWeb May 5, 2026); cross-app billing/auto-renewal trust collapse in Trustpilot reviews of MFP, Noom, Lifesum (NutriScan May 2026).
recommendations.tier_reason: UI/copy/defaults change. Streak/notification machinery from prior recommendations is preserved — only defaults flip from on → off, plus three toggle controls and one banner string. No schema or backend change. No new external API.
recommendations.why_it_matters: Partial walk-back of W20-REC-3's 'pin a visible streak counter' based on this week's evidence: the gamification trend is reversing in 2026 design discourse, MFP's streak-heavy redesign is being rejected by users, and Israeli observant users would be actively shamed by a streak that resets on Shabbat. Streaks remain available for power users who want them; they are no longer the default. Positioning the absence of streaks/ads/predatory monetization as a deliberate stance is cheap to build and a free wedge against MFP's ad-network move and Lifesum's auto-renewal complaints. From Neo's unit-economics view: zero LTV cost (no churn from shamed users), measurable trust differentiation in App Store reviews.
recommendations.user_segment: Shomer-Shabbat users (cannot log Sat → would lose streak weekly); 35+ home cooks who find streak-shaming infantilizing; trust-sensitive users churning off MFP/Lifesum in 2026.

build_hand_off.owner_agent: Agent 6 (AI) for copy + frontend team for toggles
build_hand_off.feature_flag: FF_CALM_MODE_DEFAULT (controls default state; opt-in features remain accessible regardless)
build_hand_off.schema_change: none — uses existing user_preferences (add three boolean keys, all default false)
build_hand_off.metrics: % users who toggle streaks back on (validates streak demand); D30 retention delta vs prior cohort; App Store review sentiment (Hebrew) tracked monthly

recommendations.what: Add a single user-profile flag in onboarding and settings: 'האם אתה משתמש בתרופת GLP-1 (אוזמפיק / ויגובי / מונג'ארו)?' (Are you using a GLP-1 medication?). If YES: (a) automatically raise the protein floor to 1.6 g/kg lean body mass and surface it in REC-1's widget; (b) lower the calorie floor warning threshold by 200 kcal (because appetite is suppressed and aggressive deficits risk lean-mass loss); (c) show a one-time educational card 'שמירה על מסת שריר חשובה במיוחד בתקופת GLP-1'. DO NOT store the medication name, dose, timing, or injection data. DO NOT add a medication-log surface. Add a visible disclaimer: 'איננו תחליף לייעוץ רפואי. דבר עם הרופא או התזונאי שלך.' Single boolean field only.
recommendations.inspired_by: Noom's BMI-21 microdose launch this week (eMarketer, MobiHealthNews, May 2026); MFP's GLP-1 module (W20); Shotsy 3.0 oral GLP-1 (March 2026). Israeli relevance
